title: Lappula subsessilis
intro: Lappula subsessilis, also known as the small-headed stickseed, is a plant species native to the western United States, belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in alleviating symptoms of various conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lappula subsessilis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Lappula subsessilis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Boraginaceae. The genus Lappula is part of the order Boraginales and the class Magnoliopsida. It also belongs to the division Magnoliophyta. Furthermore, the species Lappula subsessilis is a type of flowering plant that can be found in various regions. Within the Boraginaceae family, Lappula subsessilis is a species of the genus Lappula.
